phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the outdoor market opens
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure ouvre le marché en plein air ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UR OO-vruh luh mar-SHAY ahn plehn EHR
phrases.cultural_note: The Marché d'Aligre runs Tuesday through Sunday from around 8am to 1pm. It combines an outdoor flea/produce market on Place d'Aligre with the covered Marché Beauvau indoor hall (open until 1:30pm). It's considered by many Parisians the most authentic and affordable market in the city.

responses.english: The market opens around eight in the morning.
responses.phonetic: luh mar-SHAY oov-ruh vehr weet uhr doo mah-TAN
responses.french: Le marché ouvre vers huit heures du matin.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for the price of the tomatoes
phrases.text_local: C'est combien les tomates, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: seh kohm-BYEHN lay toh-MAHT, seel voo PLEH
phrases.cultural_note: The outdoor produce stalls on Place d'Aligre are known for very competitive prices, reflecting the market's working-class 12th arrondissement roots. Many vendors are of North African origin, giving the market a multicultural character with spices, olives, and preserved lemons alongside typical French produce.

responses.english: The tomatoes are three euros per kilo.
responses.phonetic: lay toh-MAHT sohn tah trwaz uh-ROH luh kee-LOH
responses.french: Les tomates sont à trois euros le kilo.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the vendor to pick the best avocados for today
phrases.text_local: Pouvez-vous me choisir les meilleurs avocats pour aujourd'hui ?
phrases.pronunciation: poo-VAY voo muh shwah-ZEER lay meh-YUR zah-voh-KAH poor oh-zhoor-DWEE
phrases.cultural_note: Vendors at Marché d'Aligre appreciate being asked for their judgment on ripeness. French market etiquette is to not touch the produce yourself — always ask the vendor to choose. This is especially appreciated at the older, family-run stalls in the covered Beauvau market.

responses.english: Here, these ones are perfect for today.
responses.phonetic: tuh-NAY, suh-LAH sohn par-FEH poor oh-zhoor-DWEE
responses.french: Tenez, ceux-là sont parfaits pour aujourd'hui.

phrases.intent_en: Browse the flea market stalls for vintage items
phrases.text_local: Y a-t-il des brocanteurs qui vendent des objets anciens ici ?
phrases.pronunciation: ee ah-TEEL day broh-kahn-TUR kee vahn day ohb-ZHEH ahn-SYEHN ee-SEE
phrases.cultural_note: The outer ring of Place d'Aligre has brocanteurs (second-hand dealers) selling vintage items, old books, records, and curiosities. The prices are significantly lower than the Puces de Saint-Ouen — this is where locals shop, not tourists, so bargaining is more relaxed.

responses.english: The flea market dealers are on Place d'Aligre.
responses.phonetic: lay broh-kahn-TUHR sohn soor lah plass dah-lee-GRUH
responses.french: Les brocanteurs sont sur la Place d'Aligre.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the indoor covered market is
phrases.text_local: Où se trouve le marché couvert Beauvau ?
phrases.pronunciation: oo suh TROOV luh mar-SHAY koo-VEHR boh-VOH
phrases.cultural_note: The Marché Beauvau is the beautiful covered hall at the heart of the Aligre complex. It specializes in top-quality meats, fish, and dairy. The cheese counters and butchers here supply many local restaurants — the quality is exceptional and prices are fair.

responses.english: The covered market is right behind the stalls.
responses.phonetic: luh mar-SHAY koo-VEHR eh zhoost deh-RYEHR layz ay-TAL
responses.french: Le marché couvert est juste derrière les étals.
